kern/syscall/dup2_syscall.c

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /*
00002  * dup2_syscall.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: June 1, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *              Piera Gueli             160119
00007  */
00008 
00009 #include <types.h>
00010 #include <kern/errno.h>
00011 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00012 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00013 #include <lib.h>
00014 #include <spl.h>
00015 #include <clock.h>
00016 #include <thread.h>
00017 #include <current.h>
00018 #include <synch.h>
00019 #include <vm.h>
00020 #include <mainbus.h>
00021 #include <vfs.h>
00022 #include <vnode.h>
00023 #include <device.h>
00024 #include <syscall.h>
00025 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00026 #include <kern/file.h>
00027 
00028 /*
00029 * dup2 clones the file handle oldfd onto the file handle newfd. If newfd names an open file, that file is closed.
00030 * The two handles refer to the same "open" of the file - that is, they are references to the same object and share the same seek * pointer. Note that this is different from opening the same file twice.
00031 * dup2 is most commonly used to relocate opened files onto STDIN_FILENO, STDOUT_FILENO, and/or STDERR_FILENO.
00032 * Both filehandles must be non-negative.
00033 * Using dup2 to clone a file handle onto itself has no effect. 
00034 */
00035 
00036 int sys_dup2(int old_fd, int new_fd, int *retval)
00037 {
00038 
00039         struct file* old_file_entry;
00040         struct file* new_file_entry;
00041         int errno;
00042 
00043         /* Check to make sure old_fd refers to a valid handle */
00044         if( curthread->table_fd[old_fd] == NULL || old_fd >=MAX_FD )
00045         {
00046                 errno = EBADF;
00047                 return errno;   
00048         }       
00049         
00050         /* Check to new_fd is a valid index of descriptor table*/
00051         if( new_fd >= MAX_FD )
00052         {
00053                 
00054                 errno = EBADF;
00055                 return errno;   
00056         }
00057         
00058         /* Check open files table size */
00059         if (curthread->last_fd == MAX_FD)
00060         {
00061                 
00062                 errno = EMFILE;
00063                 return errno;
00064         }
00065 
00066         /* Check to see if new_fd is already an open file handle */
00067         if( curthread->table_fd[new_fd] != NULL )
00068         {       
00069                 /* Get fcb using fd as index of open files table and close */
00070                 new_file_entry = curthread->table_fd[new_fd];
00071                 vfs_close(new_file_entry->vfs_node);
00072                 kfree(new_file_entry);
00073                 curthread->table_fd[new_fd] = NULL;
00074                 curthread->last_fd--;   
00075         }
00076         
00077         /* Create a file entry */ 
00078         new_file_entry = (struct file*) kmalloc(sizeof(struct file));
00079         if (new_file_entry == NULL)
00080         {
00081                 
00082                 errno = EMFILE;
00083                 return errno;
00084         }
00085         old_file_entry = curthread->table_fd[old_fd];
00086 
00087         /* Populate file entry */
00088         new_file_entry->vfs_node = old_file_entry->vfs_node;
00089         vnode_incopen(new_file_entry->vfs_node);
00090         vnode_incref(new_file_entry->vfs_node);
00091         new_file_entry->readable = old_file_entry->readable;
00092         new_file_entry->writeable = old_file_entry->writeable;
00093         
00094 
00095         /* load file entry to open files table */
00096         curthread->table_fd[new_fd] = new_file_entry;
00097         curthread->last_fd++;
00098         
00099 
00100         /* Return value to user */
00101         *retval = new_fd;
00102         
00103         return 0;
00104 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
